verses.text: David subdues the Philistines and then Moab, measuring the captives with a line. The promise that his enemies would be subdued begins to come true. What God had pledged in covenant, He now makes good in history, victory by victory.

David subdues the Philistines and then Moab, measuring the captives with a line. The promise that his enemies would be subdued begins to come true. What God had pledged in covenant, He now makes good in history, victory by victory.

David defeats Hadadezer and the Syrians who come to help him, garrisons their land, and receives tribute. The refrain sums it up: 'the LORD preserved David whithersoever he went.' His conquests are not his own strength but God's keeping hand, going with him in every direction.

David defeats Hadadezer and the Syrians who come to help him, garrisons their land, and receives tribute. The refrain sums it up: 'the LORD preserved David whithersoever he went.' His conquests are not his own strength but God's keeping hand, going with him in every direction.

David defeats Hadadezer and the Syrians who come to help him, garrisons their land, and receives tribute. The refrain sums it up: 'the LORD preserved David whithersoever he went.' His conquests are not his own strength but God's keeping hand, going with him in every direction.

David defeats Hadadezer and the Syrians who come to help him, garrisons their land, and receives tribute. The refrain sums it up: 'the LORD preserved David whithersoever he went.' His conquests are not his own strength but God's keeping hand, going with him in every direction.

David carries off shields of gold and much brass from the defeated kings. The wealth of the nations flows into Israel. What the proud gathered for themselves, God redirects to His servant and, as the next verses show, to His own house.

David carries off shields of gold and much brass from the defeated kings. The wealth of the nations flows into Israel. What the proud gathered for themselves, God redirects to His servant and, as the next verses show, to His own house.

When Toi of Hamath sends gifts, David dedicates all the silver and gold — from every nation he subdued — to the LORD. He does not hoard his spoils but consecrates them to God. The truest use of what victory brings is to lay it at God's feet.

When Toi of Hamath sends gifts, David dedicates all the silver and gold — from every nation he subdued — to the LORD. He does not hoard his spoils but consecrates them to God. The truest use of what victory brings is to lay it at God's feet.

David's fame grows, Edom is garrisoned, and again 'the LORD preserved David whithersoever he went.' Twice the refrain sounds, lest we miss it. Every triumph, in every place, is traced to one cause: not David's might, but the LORD who kept him.

David's fame grows, Edom is garrisoned, and again 'the LORD preserved David whithersoever he went.' Twice the refrain sounds, lest we miss it. Every triumph, in every place, is traced to one cause: not David's might, but the LORD who kept him.

David reigns over all Israel, executing 'judgment and justice unto all his people,' with capable officers in place. The conqueror abroad is a just ruler at home. True greatness is measured not only by victories won but by righteousness done for the people God gives us to serve.
